rulings.Case Title: MARIA LOPEZ RIVERA VS. THE FINEST JEWELRY.COM INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Award Of Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA's Motion For An Award Of Attorney Fees Motion granted. Plaintiff achieved her litigation objection in her Motion for Entry of Judgment and is thus considered the "prevailing party" pursuant to the parties' Settlement Agreement. Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal.4th 599, 603-604 (holding the defendants were the "prevailing party" under the parties' real estate agreement when the plaintiffs voluntarily dismissed the action because the defendants' litigation objective was to prevent the plaintiffs from obtaining relief). Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $6,067.50, plus costs of $520.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOUVEIA et al VS. JAMES LITTRELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Second And Third Set Of Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTS JAMES LITTRELL, LITTRELL, J., DBA RUBY LIVING, LLC and INTERIOR MOVES, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Answers To Second And Third Set Of Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: : Parties to appear. Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing and advise the Court of any further progress on agreement to supplemental responses. Further verified supplemental responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 14 and 16 must be served on or before October 24, 2014. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIFFANY D NOVOTNY VS. NEXANT INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS NEXANT INC., DEBORAH RIMMLER, AN INDIVIDUAL, CHRISTINE HARPER and KAREN NEUENDORFF's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendants met their initial burden showing there are no triable issues of material fact. The only evidence submitted in opposition is Plaintiff's declaration. The declaration is largely conclusory and speculative. The declaration does not give rise to any triable issues of material fact. As to the discrimination and wrongful termination claims, Defendant met their burden of showing that there were legitimate business reasons for Plaintiff's termination - Plaintiff's unsatisfactory performance regarding her interactions with co-workers and Plaintiff's medical condition making her unable to work for an indefinite period of time. (Defendants' Issue No. 3, Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 12-17; Issue No. 10, UMF 1-7.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of producing "substantial, responsive evidence that the employer's showing was untrue or pretextual." (Hersant v. Cal. Dept. of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-05.) As to the harassment and hostile work environment claims, Defendants met their initial burden showing that the alleged acts of harassment were not severe or pervasive. (Issue No. 9, UMF 1-14.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of raising a triable issue of material fact, as her declaration only states specific facts about a few incidences. Acts of harassment cannot be "occasional, isolated, sporadic, or trivial." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1043.) The complaint alleges that Plaintiff was retaliated against pursuant to Cal. Lab. Code secs. 1102.5 and 2856. (Complaint, par. 95.) Defendants met their burden showing Plaintiff cannot establish the material elements of the claim. (Issue 13, UMF 1-5.) Plaintiff's declaration states no facts to the contrary. As to the breach of contract and breach of covenant of good faith causes of action, Defendants met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiff cannot set forth facts to establish this cause of action. (Issue 8, UMF 1-14.) Plaintiff's declaration does not set forth facts to the contrary, specifically because Plaintiff's declaration does not state any facts regarding the existence of a contract. Defendants met their initial burden on the cause of action for negligent retention and supervision. (Issue 6, UMF 1-8.) Plaintiff's declaration sets forth no facts that the employer knew or should have known that an employee presented an undue harm to third persons. (Federico v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1207, 1214.) As to the promissory fraud and negligent misrepresentation claims, Defendants met their initial burden. (Issue 14, UMF 1-6; Issue 16, UMF 1-7.) Plaintiff's declaration states no facts that establish that Defendants did not perform as promised. Defendants met their initial burden on the IIED claim. (Issue 15, 1-8.) Plaintiff's declaration do not state facts sufficient to give rise to a triable issue that Defendants' engaged in conduct "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized community." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050.) Lastly, the negligence claim fails as a matter of law because it is barred by the exclusivity provision of the Worker's Compensation Act. (Fermino v. Fedco (1994) 7 Cal.4th 701, 714-15.) (end of part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT ALVARADO VS. MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORP. and CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORP.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Compel the production of plaintiff ALVARADO's psychiatric records is DENIED. Defendants have failed to establish that the documents and information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The copy of the Le Declaration submitted to the Court was not signed, and was therefore not considered by the Court. Defendants' request for sanctions in an unspecified amount is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA VS. GILBANE BUILDING COMPANY, A RHODE ISLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANT NOVA PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Demurrer is sustained as to the negligence cause of action with ten days leave for Cross-Complainants to allege facts establishing that Nova Partners owed a duty to Cross-Complainants. The Demurrer to the remaining causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. As to Cross-Complainant Travelers, the claim for breach of performance bond in the underlying Complaint does not support causes of action for equitable relief from Cross-Defendant. As to Cross-Complainant Gilbane, the Complaint fails to allege tort causes of action against it that would permit Gilbane to seek equitable relief from Cross-Defendant Nova. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
